$3.25 trillion

that's how much trading volume has now relied on Pyth's price data

every time someone trades crypto, tokenized stocks, commodities, or other assets, the exchange needs to know the correct price

that's what @PythNetwork does

in May, apps using Pyth processed $130B in trading volume

In June, they've already added another $100B

that brings the total value of trades powered by Pyth to over $3.25 trillion

a growing share of that volume comes from RWA perpetuals - markets based on real-world assets like stocks, commodities, and indices

the bigger these markets become, the more important reliable price data becomes

Pyth isn't just an oracle anymore - it's becoming the infrastructure behind multi asset trading

you sign up, drop your email, start trading in a sandbox, and you're one click away from producing marketing material

Google fired the engineer who built their most useful tool of the year

Justin Poehnelt spent almost 7 years at Google on the Workspace DevRel team, then he built a CLI for Workspace - unified terminal access to Gmail, Drive, Calendar, all of it

it hit #1 on Hacker News, thousands of GitHub stars, thousands of real users within days, directors were reaching out asking what they could learn from it.

then legal got involved over branding, then he got fired

the irony: Google announced their own official Workspace CLI at Cloud Next just two days before he was let go

his take - it wasn't really about the branding, it was Workspace leadership being nervous about what AI agents mean for their product

sometimes the org doesn't fear the competitor, it fears its own employee moving faster than it can

> new reporting confirms: Amazon found the jailbreak, Jassy (Amazon CEO) escalated it straight to the White House, and Trump advisor David Sacks publicly says Anthropic knew and sat on it. Anthropic disputes this, says it only got verbal, narrow evidence

> but here's the part that matters beyond this week - the order doesn't just hit "hostile" countries

it blocks ALL foreign nationals, everywhere, including UK, Canada, EU citizens. Anthropic's own foreign-born employees in the US lost access too

that's the headline buried in this story: US frontier AI models can now be switched off as instruments of national power, not treated as global infrastructure

even allied governments got caught in this

the UK's AI minister already used it as a pitch for domestic AI investment

this is exactly the "AI sovereignty" argument people have been making for years, except now it's not theoretical

other labs (OpenAI, Google) explicitly weren't touched by this directive. Anthropic-specific, at least for now

still down as of today

if you're betting on this resolving cleanly and fast

One developer launched a $5.99 game

13 days later it had sold 7,000,000 copies

that's roughly $42 million in sales before Steam takes its cut

the game is called MECCHA CHAMELEON

MECCHA CHAMELEON might be the most insane indie success story of 2026

a few weeks ago almost nobody had heard of it

today it's one of Steam's biggest viral hits

so what happened?

> the creator, known as lemorion_1224, built a simple idea around one question:

"What if hide-and-seek worked like a chameleon?"

players paint their white character to perfectly match the environment and then freeze in place while others try to spot them

> the gameplay sounds ridiculously simple

but that's exactly why it works

every match creates hilarious moments, impossible disguises, and viral clips that spread naturally across social media

> the game launched on Steam on June 9, 2026

low friction
easy to convince friends
perfect streamer bait

> steam reviews quickly turned "Very Positive."

players compared it to Prop Hunt, but with a completely new camouflage mechanic

> then the numbers exploded

reports showed over 3 million copies sold within roughly the first week after launch, pushing the game to the top of Steam's global sales charts

> the craziest part?

the project is widely reported as being built primarily by a solo Japanese developer with help from artist Haganeiro

> the lesson isn't "make a clone."

the lesson is that simple ideas are still underrated

one memorable mechanic
instantly understandable gameplay
infinite funny moments

that's a formula the internet loves

MECCHA CHAMELION went from unknown indie game to a global phenomenon almost overnight

sometimes one great idea is enough

RT @hammertime_one: CLAUDE FABLE 5 WAS TOP 1 ON EVERY LEADERBOARD FOR EXACTLY 72 HOURS

released june 9. by june 11 it was TOP 1 ON ARTIFIC…

CLAUDE FABLE 5 WAS TOP 1 ON EVERY LEADERBOARD FOR EXACTLY 72 HOURS

released june 9. by june 11 it was TOP 1 ON ARTIFICIAL ANALYSIS INDEX, #1 ON LMARENA, #1 ON SWE-BENCH PRO AT 80.3% (next closest model, opus 4.8, at 69.2%. GPT-5.5 at 58.6%)

june 12: US EXPORT-CONTROL DIRECTIVE

Anthropic pulls Fable 5 and Mythos 5 FOR EVERY USER ON THE PLANET, no notice, no opt-out, no refund mechanism

the reason it had to be global and not just "blocked for foreign nationals" as written: Anthropic CAN'T VERIFY A USER'S NATIONALITY IN REAL TIME at the api layer

so the only compliant move was killing it for everyone, INCLUDING PAYING US CUSTOMERS AND ANTHROPIC'S OWN STAFF

the part worth remembering past this specific story: your access to any cloud model sits at the intersection of two things YOU DON'T CONTROL - the vendor's business calls and a regulator's reach

paying for it changes nothing about that

BEST MODEL ON EARTH, GONE IN 72 HOURS, BY DIRECTIVE

what actually happened:

Fable 5 launched June 9 - the most capable public AI model ever

June 12 - US Department of Commerce banned it, national security

Polymarket gives July 1 return a 47% chance
$1.4M in total volume on this question

Bloomberg: the Trump admin's move raises unprecedented legal questions

President has since called Anthropic "no longer a national-security threat" negotiations are ongoing

so the bet is: can Anthropic close a deal with the government in 9 days?

do you think Fable 5 comes back by July 1?

Fable 5 is Anthropic's most powerful public model to date

but the real story isn't capability anymore

It's economics

firms aren't asking "is it smarter?"

they're asking "does it replace a $200k engineer workflow for $20/month?"

Anthropic is still shipping it with guardrails:
> high-risk cyber queries routed to older Opus-class models
> bio/chem restricted by design
> safety triggers in <5% of sessions (company reported)

this is what frontier AI looks like now:
> not just intelligence gains
> but controlled intelligence inside a pricing war
> benchmarks don't decide winners anymore

unit economics do

2025: the AI race was about benchmarks

2026: it's about jurisdiction

Fable 5 launched as the clear SOTA model

Karpathy called it "SOTA on everything by a margin."
72 hours later, it was gone under a U.S. export directive

but Polymarket still has Anthropic as the overwhelming favorite to finish June on top -
[https://t.co/qxkByyU2D4]

the market just priced in a weird reality:

you can ban distribution

you can't un-invent capability

who's next to move the frontier?
